sentences.quote: We present the first formally verified derivation that the number of Standard-Model fermion generations satisfies $N_f \equiv 0 \pmod{3}$, following the global modular constraint of Wang~\cite{Wang2024}.
sentences.quote_normalized: we present the first formally verified derivation that the number of standard-model fermion generations satisfies $n_f \equiv 0 \pmod{3}$, following the global modular constraint of wang~.
sentences.agent_notes: R3 unchanged from R2. First-claim spot-check (literature registries: Coq/Lean/Agda/Isabelle community archives have no prior formalization of N_f ≡ 0 mod 3 from modular invariance). PASS at registry-spot-check confidence; appropriately hedged in body (§IV uses 'We are not aware of any prior...').

sentences.quote: The chain combines two independent ingredients machine-checked in Lean~4: the chiral central charge $c_- = 8 N_f$ derived from $16$ left-handed Weyl components per generation in the with-$\nu_R$ convention, and the framing-anomaly constraint $24 \mid c_-$ following from $\eta(\tau+1) = e^{2\pi i/24}\,\eta(\tau)$ via Mathlib's \texttt{Complex.exp\_add}.
sentences.agent_notes: R3 unchanged from R2. All four cited theorems exist (verified via direct grep on lean/SKEFTHawking/{WangBridge,ModularInvarianceConstraint,SMFermionData}.lean). 8 = 16 × 1/2 (Weyl-fermion central-charge contribution per AlvarezGaumeWitten1984) is arithmetic; 24 from Dedekind-η phase via Complex.exp_add is structurally correct.
R3 unchanged from R2. N_f=3 is the minimal positive integer divisible by 3; arithmetically tautological. Matches generation_mod3_constraint.

sentences.quote: Without right-handed neutrinos, $c_- = 15/2$ is fractional, giving a formal independent argument for $\nu_R$ from modular invariance alone.
sentences.agent_notes: R3 unchanged from R2. Theorem central_charge_fractional_without_nu_R exists at WangBridge.lean:109. 15/2 is non-integer (15 odd); Lean encodes via contradiction from 15/2 ∈ ℕ.

sentences.quote: The algebraic core of the Rokhlin bound is verified through homological degree~5 by an explicit minimal free resolution $\dim \mathrm{Ext}^n_{A(1)}(\mathbb{F}_2,\mathbb{F}_2) = 1,2,2,2,3,4$ together with a change-of-rings adjunction $\mathrm{Hom}_A(A \otimes_{A(1)} M, N) \cong \mathrm{Hom}_{A(1)}(M, N)$, the first such computation in any proof assistant.
sentences.agent_notes: R3 unchanged from R2. Ext dimensions 1,2,2,2,3,4 confirmed in lean/SKEFTHawking/A1Ext.lean (ext_dim_0..ext_dim_5).

sentences.quote: The verification is fully automated by \texttt{lake build} on a $5812$-theorem $322$-module library with one axiom and zero \texttt{sorry}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the verification is fully automated by lake build on a $5812$-theorem $322$-module library with one axiom and zero sorry.

sentences.quote: Wang~\cite{Wang2024} proposed that a \emph{global} modular constraint --- $24 \mid c_-$ on the chiral central charge of the partition function under the modular $T$-transformation $\tau \to \tau+1$ --- combined with the SM's fermion content $c_- = 8 N_f$, forces $N_f \equiv 0 \pmod{3}$.
sentences.agent_notes: R3 unchanged from R2. Wang2024 (Phys. Rev. D 110, 125028; arXiv:2312.14928) confirmed in CITATION_REGISTRY.

sentences.quote: The coefficient $8$ in $c_- = 8 N_f$ is therefore not free: it is \emph{derived} from the count \texttt{total\_components\_with\_nu\_R} = 16 by the Lean theorem \texttt{WangBridge.fermion\_count\_gives\_central\_charge}.
sentences.agent_notes: R3 unchanged from R2. Both theorems exist (WangBridge.lean:73 / SMFermionData.lean:176).

sentences.quote: Modular invariance demands $e^{2\pi i c_-/24} = 1$, equivalently $24 \mid c_-$, formalized as \texttt{ModularInvarianceConstraint.framing\_anomaly\_constraint}. Combining with $c_- = 8 N_f$: $24 \mid 8 N_f \Longleftrightarrow 3 \mid N_f$.
sentences.agent_notes: R3 unchanged from R2. framing_anomaly_constraint exists at ModularInvarianceConstraint.lean:103. generation_constraint_iff at GenerationConstraint.lean:106.
R3 unchanged from R2. Six dimensions match A1Ext.lean ext_dim_n theorems for n=0..5 (1,2,2,2,3,4).

sentences.quote: We are not aware of any prior machine-checked Ext computation over a Steenrod sub-algebra in any proof assistant.
sentences.agent_notes: R3 unchanged from R2. Hedged language ('We are not aware') is appropriately conservative.

sentences.quote: Combining $c_- = 8 N_f$ from SM fermion counting with $24 \mid c_-$ from Dedekind-eta modular invariance gives $N_f \equiv 0 \pmod{3}$, with $N_f = 3$ as the minimal nontrivial solution.
sentences.agent_notes: R3 unchanged from R2. Headline conclusion claim. Cross-bundle bridge to D2 §2 (same chain, same Lean theorems) — consistent.

sentences.quote: The chain is machine-checked end to end: the coefficient $8$ via Mathlib finite sums in \texttt{SMFermionData.lean}; the modular factor $24$ via \texttt{Complex.exp\_add} in \texttt{ModularInvarianceConstraint.lean}; the divisibility step via \texttt{Nat.Coprime.dvd\_of\_dvd\_mul\_left} in \texttt{GenerationConstraint.lean}; the algebraic Rokhlin core via the explicit minimal free resolution in \texttt{A1Ring.lean}, \texttt{A1Resolution.lean}, and \texttt{A1Ext.lean}; and the change-of-rings step in \texttt{ChangeOfRings.lean}.
sentences.agent_notes: R3 unchanged from R2. All seven Lean modules exist in lean/SKEFTHawking/.

sentences.quote: The $24 \mid c_-$ constraint admits a deeper structural reading: the five-step chain spin-bordism $\Omega_5^{\mathrm{Spin}}(BG_{\mathrm{SM}})$ $\to$ anomaly polynomial $\to$ modular invariance $\to$ Niemeier-lattice classification~\cite{Niemeier1973} $\to$ Schellekens' classification of meromorphic $c=24$ CFTs~\cite{Schellekens1993}, geometrically completed for the strongly-rational, weight-1-non-trivial subset by M\"oller--Scheithauer~\cite{MollerScheithauer2024}, places $24 \mid c_-$ as a corollary of holomorphic-VOA classification rather than a stand-alone modular-consistency requirement; the deep companion paper~\cite{Roehm2026D2} carries the full derivation.
sentences.agent_notes: R3 verification of R2 BLOCKER #1 fix: \cite{Roehm2026Wave10} (R2 self-cite circular) → \cite{Roehm2026D2} (correct deep-companion). Verified: (a) Roehm2026D2 present at line 293 (cite) and line 383 (\bibitem); (b) CITATION_REGISTRY contains 'Roehm2026D2' entry at src/core/citations.py:7317 with 'in_preparation' status (D2 deep companion to L2, 2026); (c) two pdflatex passes with no 'undefined citation' warnings (4pp/431795 bytes). Sentence-id hash unchanged (39dd2303) because \cite{...} normalizes to empty in quote_normalized; the cite-target swap is captured in chain_proposed.links. WARN preserved on Möller-Scheithauer scope hedge alignment with D2 §2.7 — see followup:L2-r2-1 carried forward to R3.

sentences.quote: The without-$\nu_R$ argument upgrades from suggestive to formal: a fractional central charge $c_- = 15/2$ is gravitationally anomalous; no fractional natural number exists; therefore $\nu_R$ exists if the modular framing-anomaly constraint applies to the SM.
sentences.agent_notes: R3 unchanged from R2. Both theorems exist (WangBridge.lean:109, RokhlinBridge.lean:265).

sentences.quote: The full verification builds in $\sim 30~\mathrm{s}$ on commodity hardware under Lean~4 v4.29.0 against Mathlib commit \texttt{8850ed93} (library state: 5812 theorems across 322 modules, 1 axiom \texttt{gapped\_interface\_axiom} unused in this chain, zero \texttt{sorry}).
